about.subtitle: We are a team of senior advisors, technologists, and operators dedicated to helping businesses reach their peak potential.

story.headline: Our Story
story.content:
Founded in 2014, Summit Consulting was born from a simple observation: too many businesses invest in strategy without the operational muscle to execute it. Our founders, each with 20+ years in Fortune 500 leadership, set out to build a firm that bridges the gap between vision and execution.

Today, we serve over 150 clients across technology, finance, retail, and manufacturing — delivering measurable results that compound year over year.

blogPost.title: 5 Signs Your Business Needs a Digital Transformation
blogPost.author: Marcus Rivera
blogPost.content:
Introduction

Digital transformation is more than a buzzword. It is the process of fundamentally rethinking how your business uses technology, people, and processes to drive better outcomes. After working with dozens of companies on their transformation journeys, we have identified five unmistakable signs that it is time to act.

Sign 1: Manual Processes Are Slowing You Down

If your team spends more time on spreadsheets than on strategy, something is wrong. Manual data entry, paper-based approvals, and disconnected systems are not just inefficient — they are a competitive liability.

What to Look For

Count the number of times data is manually transferred between systems. If the answer is more than twice per day, you have a clear opportunity for automation.

Sign 2: Customer Expectations Are Outpacing Your Capabilities

Today's customers expect real-time updates, self-service portals, and seamless cross-channel experiences. If your competitors offer these and you do not, you are losing deals you never even knew about.

The Customer Experience Gap

Survey your customers quarterly. If satisfaction scores are declining while competitor reviews improve, the gap is widening and technology is almost always the root cause.

Sign 3: Your Data Lives in Silos

When sales cannot see what marketing is doing, and operations cannot access customer feedback, your data is working against you instead of for you.

Sign 4: Innovation Has Stalled

If your last major product or service improvement was more than two years ago, it is a sign that your technology stack and processes are holding back innovation rather than enabling it.

Sign 5: Scaling Requires Proportional Headcount Growth

A truly digital business can scale revenue without proportional increases in headcount. If doubling your revenue requires doubling your team, your systems are not doing their job.

The Scalability Test

Calculate your revenue per employee over the last three years. If it is flat or declining, technology investment is overdue.

What to Do Next

Recognizing the signs is the first step. The next step is building a roadmap that addresses your specific situation without disrupting the operations that keep your business running today. That is where we come in.

items.question: Is the discovery session really free?
items.answer: Yes. We offer a complimentary 60-minute discovery session to understand your challenges and recommend the right approach. No commitment required.

items.question: Can I switch plans mid-engagement?
items.answer: Absolutely. We can upgrade or adjust your engagement model at any time based on evolving needs.

items.question: Do you offer non-profit or startup discounts?
items.answer: Yes. We offer reduced rates for qualified non-profits and early-stage startups. Contact us to discuss your situation.

legal.title: Terms & Privacy
legal.headline: Terms of Service & Privacy Policy
legal.terms:
1. Acceptance of Terms

By accessing and using the Summit Consulting website and services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our services.

2. Services

Summit Consulting provides business advisory, consulting, and related professional services. The specific scope of services will be defined in individual engagement agreements.

3. Intellectual Property

All materials, methodologies, frameworks, and deliverables created by Summit Consulting remain our intellectual property unless explicitly transferred in writing as part of an engagement agreement.

4. Confidentiality

We treat all client information as confidential and will not disclose it to third parties without your written consent, except as required by law.

5. Limitation of Liability

Summit Consulting's liability is limited to the fees paid for the specific engagement giving rise to the claim. We are not liable for indirect, consequential, or punitive damages.
legal.privacy:
1. Information We Collect

We collect information you provide directly, including your name, email address, phone number, and company information when you contact us or engage our services.

2. How We Use Your Information

We use your information to provide and improve our services, communicate with you about engagements, and send relevant industry insights (with your consent).

3. Data Protection

We implement industry-standard security measures to protect your personal information. We do not sell your data to third parties.

4. Cookies

Our website uses essential cookies for functionality and analytics cookies to improve user experience. You can opt out of analytics cookies through your browser settings.

5. Your Rights

You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal information at any time. Contact us at privacy@summit-consulting.com to exercise these rights.
